title: Yui Ikari Sadist
wikitext:
[]: Yui Ikari: Sadist? Post by The Goose » Sun Jun 22, 2008 11:11 pm Its common knownledge for any ultimate Eva fan that Yui Ikari was trying to become one with Eva Unit-01 during the experiment that horried Shinji. She says that she wants to let Shinji see "how bright the future will be", but if she knew that the experiment would result in her absorbtion, why did she let Shinji watch? Did she think that letting three year old Shinji watch his only mother get taken away from him was a happy moment in her son's future? Or was she getting kicks out his torment?

[]: Post by slothen » Sun Jun 22, 2008 11:35 pm did yui know what would happen? I would guess Gendou did not know.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jun 23, 2008 12:03 am Did you actually pay attention to the series? She wanted him to know she was there when the time was right. She did it so he, and everyone for that matter, would have a future.

[]: Post by Skywalker007 » Mon Jun 23, 2008 1:45 am Quite a bright future indeed: Everybody dead, her husband turn to be a maniac psycho, her son mind has broke down to pieces, and earth become a red lifeless planet. But no, I don't think she is a sadist. She doesn't created a plan at all. If she does have a plan, Gendo and Shinji would be very much better prepare. Anyway, I wonder that why Yui doesn't comfort Shinji before he goes killing everyone. Why does she have to appear after everyone is already dead?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 23, 2008 2:02 am Skywalker007 wrote: Quite a bright future indeed: Everybody dead, her husband turn to be a maniac psycho, her son mind has broke down to pieces, and earth become a red lifeless planet.

[]: Post by The Goose » Mon Jun 23, 2008 3:50 am Did you actually pay attention to the series? She wanted him to know she was there when the time was right. She did it so he, and everyone for that matter, would have a future.

[]: Post by Zerogoki » Mon Jun 23, 2008 10:38 am The Goose wrote: I get that she was being aborbed for the greater good, but where does she get the right for showing her own son a tramua inducing sight of seeing his own mother getting absorbed? I can understand letting him know where she is, but did she have to let him know by letting him watch a horrifing sight like that? Couldn't she have left a note or have Kozo or Gendo tell him where she is? She didn't have to scare her son like that and leave a huge mental scar on him.

[]: Post by The Goose » Mon Jun 23, 2008 11:25 am I guess thats true, but Yui bringing Shinji to that experiment still seems like bad parenting.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 23, 2008 12:07 pm Quite a bright future indeed: Everybody dead, her husband turn to be a maniac psycho, her son mind has broke down to pieces, and earth become a red lifeless planet.

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Jun 23, 2008 12:49 pm slothen wrote: did yui know what would happen? I would guess Gendou did not know.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 23, 2008 3:55 pm Mr. Tines wrote: But there are incurable optimists around here who still believe that a bunch of SF fen did not know what they were doing when they showed GNR (and any reasonable hope) falling to pieces.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 23, 2008 4:01 pm Reichu wrote: Every time Mr. Tines brings this up in a thread, [complete the meme]...

[]: Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jun 23, 2008 4:05 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Reichu wrote: Every time Mr. Tines brings this up in a thread, [complete the meme]...

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Jun 23, 2008 7:37 pm Reichu wrote: She's talking to some random Gehirn dude, not Gendo. [IMAGE]

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 23, 2008 10:39 pm master_lloyd wrote: FIX'D (and see below in sig)

[]: Post by Skywalker007 » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:59 am Not at all. You can see perfectly intact plant-life and whatnot no problem, and everyone is going to come back to life as the planet's ecosystem restores itself. The state we see Earth in at the end is only temporary.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:10 pm Skywalker007 wrote: less sure on the theory that the ecosystem may restore itself.

[]: Post by Xard » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:22 pm Not to forget we see trees etc. in Tines favourite pic too, heh Don't misunderstand me, world is fucked up BAD after Third Impact. It's just there's no basis for thinking damage is permanent.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:24 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: And also: "Everytime Tines-Ojichan brings up the "Ecosystem is fucked" POV, SSD's head explodes."

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 24, 2008 5:26 pm ^
